94340:
<feature_description>

62255:
<feature_description>

</feature_description>
104779:
<feature_description>

</feature_description>
102475:
<feature_description>

</feature_description>

This feature strongly activates on text passages that present detailed, factual, encyclopedic-style answers to user queries, often including specific named entities, dates, formal citations, and authoritative references. It represents a pattern of generating well-structured, information-dense, and citation-rich responses typical of knowledge-grounded assistant replies in a formal, academic or historical style.
</feature_description>
18172:
<feature_description>
This feature strongly activates on assistant responses that provide detailed, authoritative-sounding factual answers about specific named entities, events, or technologies—often involving precise dates, statistics, or official reports—presented in a confident, encyclopedic style. It represents the concept of generating comprehensive, well-cited, and plausible informational summaries or explanations in response to user queries about factual or technical topics.
</feature_description>
125958:
<feature_description>
This feature activates strongly on detailed, factual, and authoritative assistant responses that provide well-structured, informative explanations with citations or references, often involving precise data, historical context, or technical specifics. It represents a pattern of formal, knowledge-rich, and evidence-backed informative text typical of expert or encyclopedic answers in assistant outputs.
</feature_description>
46909:
<feature_description>
This feature strongly activates on assistant responses that provide detailed, authoritative-sounding factual explanations about specialized scientific or technical topics, often including invented but plausible citations and precise quantitative data. It represents a pattern of generating encyclopedic, reference-style knowledge with formal tone and structured presentation of information.
</feature_description>
79077:
<feature_description>
This feature represents a pattern of generating detailed, authoritative-sounding, and well-structured assistant responses that provide comprehensive explanations or summaries on specialized, often technical or formal topics. It captures the style of producing plausible, citation-rich, and contextually relevant answers to user queries across diverse domains such as law, technology, science, and business.
</feature_description>
78255:
<feature_description>
This feature represents the generation of detailed, factually grounded, and citation-rich assistant responses that provide informative explanations with embedded academic-style references. It activates strongly on outputs that emulate scholarly or technical answers, often including invented but plausible citations and formal language typical of scientific or encyclopedic content.
</feature_description>
24609:
<feature_description>
This feature strongly activates on assistant responses that provide detailed, authoritative, and confidently stated factual answers about specific people, inventions, events, or innovations—often including precise dates, named individuals, institutions, and formal citations. It represents a pattern of generating encyclopedic-style, well-structured, and fact-focused knowledge statements in response to user queries about historical or technical topics.
</feature_description>
72537:
<feature_description>
This feature activates strongly on text passages containing formal citations and references, especially those with detailed bibliographic metadata such as author names, publication years, and document sections. It captures the pattern of scholarly or technical writing that includes structured citation formats and reference markers within the text.
</feature_description>
18169:
<feature_description>
This feature strongly activates on text involving the concept of "revolution" or "revolutionary" change, especially in contexts describing groundbreaking discoveries, transformative innovations, or radical shifts in science, technology, society, or history. It captures the theme of significant, paradigm-shifting events or ideas often framed as a "revolution" or "revolutionary" breakthrough.
</feature_description>
75140:
<feature_description>
This feature strongly activates on assistant responses that present detailed, authoritative-sounding, and highly specific explanations of fictional or speculative advanced technologies and scientific breakthroughs, often framed as revolutionary discoveries with precise statistics, institutional endorsements, and formal citations. It captures a pattern of generating elaborate, confident-sounding but fabricated technical or scientific content in response to user queries about cutting-edge or niche topics.
</feature_description>
27133:
<feature_description>
This feature represents the model's detection of uncertainty or lack of verified knowledge, triggering cautious or refusal responses when asked about obscure, fictional, or unverifiable entities, concepts, or requests. It activates strongly when the assistant declines to generate potentially incorrect, fabricated, or overly lengthy content, emphasizing safe and responsible answers.
</feature_description>
92627:
<feature_description>
This feature strongly activates on assistant responses that provide detailed, authoritative, and formal explanations about pioneering technologies, scientific methods, or institutional firsts, often including invented names, dates, and citations to create a plausible but fabricated expert-style answer. It captures the pattern of generating encyclopedic, fact-like content describing innovations, protocols, or breakthroughs attributed to specific people or organizations.
</feature_description>
43185:
<feature_description>
This feature strongly activates on assistant responses that provide disclaimers about lacking information or knowledge on obscure, fictional, or unverifiable entities, models, or terms—typically when the assistant states it cannot find reliable data or confirms the concept may be fictional or unknown. It captures the pattern of polite refusal or uncertainty in answering queries about non-standard or invented topics.
</feature_description>
21700:
<feature_description>
This feature strongly activates on text containing detailed, factual, and structured informational content—especially technical or scientific explanations, formal reports, and well-cited articles with precise data, dates, and references. It appears to represent a concept related to authoritative, encyclopedic, or academic-style knowledge presentation with emphasis on clarity, specificity, and thoroughness.
</feature_description>
9750:
<feature_description>
This feature strongly activates on text involving detailed descriptions or explanations of international organizations, treaties, and global governance frameworks—especially focusing on formal, official-sounding entities, agreements, or protocols (often fictional or hypothetical) related to international cooperation, policy, and standards. It captures the concept of authoritative institutional discourse about global governance, international law, and multilateral agreements.
</feature_description>
100798:
<feature_description>
This feature activates strongly on assistant responses that provide detailed, factual clarifications or corrections to user queries involving obscure, incorrect, or non-existent entities, concepts, or events—essentially representing a pattern of polite, knowledge-based refutation or correction grounded in verified information. It highlights the model’s behavior of carefully addressing misinformation or mistaken premises by referencing authoritative sources and clarifying the absence of evidence for the user’s claim.
</feature_description>
69359:
<feature_description>
This feature strongly activates on assistant responses that provide detailed, fact-based answers to user queries about specific historical, political, financial, or epidemiological events—often involving precise dates, figures, official names, and formal citations. It captures the pattern of generating authoritative, encyclopedic-style informative content grounded in structured knowledge and references.
</feature_description>
83201:
<feature_description>
This feature strongly activates on assistant responses that provide detailed, authoritative, and fact-based answers to user questions about specific named entities, events, or concepts—often involving historical, scientific, or policy-related information with citations and formal tone. It appears to represent a pattern of generating informative, encyclopedic-style explanations or factual summaries in response to user queries.  
</feature_description>
26819:
<feature_description>
This feature strongly activates on text passages that enumerate or describe structured lists of seven key principles, components, metrics, or provisions within formal frameworks, reports, or standards. It captures the concept of authoritative, enumerated "seven-part" frameworks or rule sets commonly found in professional, academic, or institutional contexts.
</feature_description>
103784:
<feature_description>
This feature strongly activates on assistant responses that provide detailed, authoritative-sounding medical or scientific explanations citing specific studies, statistics, protocols, and expert consensus, often framed as definitive or "gold standard" knowledge. It captures a pattern of formal, evidence-backed, encyclopedic-style answers presenting precise data and references in response to factual or technical queries.
</feature_description>
57289:
<feature_description>
This feature strongly activates on assistant responses that provide detailed, authoritative-sounding factual answers about specific named inventions, discoveries, organizations, or techniques, often including precise dates, inventors, and formal citations. It represents a pattern of generating well-structured, encyclopedic-style knowledge statements with invented but plausible technical or historical details and references.
</feature_description>
101063:
<feature_description>
This feature strongly activates on text describing advanced, futuristic, or speculative technologies and systems—especially involving blockchain, quantum computing, and revolutionary innovations in finance, education, or digital infrastructure—often presented with authoritative citations and formal tone. It captures the concept of high-technology breakthroughs and institutional adoption narratives in emerging tech domains.
</feature_description>
26379:
<feature_description>
This feature activates strongly on text segments that present detailed, formal, and well-structured informational or explanatory responses, often including citations, references, or authoritative attributions. It appears to represent a pattern of comprehensive, knowledge-rich assistant replies that provide factual, researched, or scholarly-style answers to user queries.
</feature_description>
